settings.question: Who is this actually for?
settings.answer:
People who sweat. A lot.

Athletes, fighters, endurance runners, hot climate trainers, heavy sweaters, and anyone who has tried hydration products and still felt flat. If your workouts are light and air-conditioned, you probably do not need this and that is fine.

settings.question: Why is there so much salt in this?
settings.answer:
Because sweat is not just water. It is mostly sodium.

When you train hard or sweat heavily, you are losing electrolytes and sodium is the primary one. Low sodium drinks are fine for light movement. This formula is built for people who actually empty their sweat glands.

settings.question: Isn't high sodium bad for you?
settings.answer:
Only when context is ignored, which happens a lot.

High sodium can be a problem in sedentary, ultra processed diets. For athletes, heavy sweaters, and people training in heat, not getting enough sodium is often the real performance issue. This product is designed for that reality.

settings.question: Is this just expensive salt water?
settings.answer:
If it were, we would not be proud of it and you would not feel great drinking it.

This delivers about one thousand milligrams of sodium on purpose along with real potassium and magnesium, zero sugar, no maltodextrin, and a flavor system you will actually finish. Salt water is cheap. Performance hydration is not.

settings.question: Why is this so expensive compared to other electrolyte mixes?
settings.answer:
Because we did not build it to be cheap. We built it to work.

Many high sodium competitors underdose magnesium. Sports drinks rely on sugar to do the heavy lifting. Low sodium wellness mixes prioritize vibes over performance. A lot of brands save money with maltodextrin carriers, stevia overload, or proprietary blends.

We chose high sodium plus meaningful potassium and magnesium, organic ingredients wherever possible, maltodextrin-free flavors, zero sugar, no stevia, no sugar alcohols, full transparency down to the milligram, and third-party banned substance testing.

You are not paying for hype. You are paying for what is actually inside and what we intentionally left out.

settings.question: Why no sugar? Do I not need carbs?
settings.answer:
Sometimes you do. Not always and not here.

This is a hydration tool, not a juice box. Sugar can help with long endurance fueling, but it is not required for electrolyte replacement. We did not want to force sugar into every bottle. Fuel separately if you need to. This handles hydration cleanly.

settings.question: Why monk fruit instead of stevia or sucralose?
settings.answer:
Because taste and tolerance matter.

Monk fruit gives clean sweetness without the metallic aftertaste of stevia, without diet soda vibes, and without digestive roulette from sugar alcohols. It does its job and then politely disappears.

settings.question: What is the deal with natural flavors?
settings.answer:
They make the drink actually drinkable.

Electrolytes at this sodium level are brutal without a real flavor system. Ours are organic, maltodextrin-free, and designed specifically for high sodium performance hydration. You could drink salt water. We just did not think you would enjoy it.

settings.question: Why include magnesium at all?
settings.answer:
Because muscles and nerves do not run on sodium alone.

Magnesium supports muscle function, nerve signaling, and energy metabolism. Many mixes either skip it entirely or include a token amount. We did not.

settings.question: Why this form of magnesium?
settings.answer:
Because form matters.

Dimagnesium malate delivers a meaningful dose while staying gentle enough for regular use. It is here to support performance, not decorate the label.

settings.question: What is bamboo extract doing in my electrolyte stick?
settings.answer:
Solving a real problem quietly.

It keeps the powder flowing so your stick actually pours and mixes. It also provides silica, which supports connective tissue. We chose a plant-derived solution instead of synthetic filler shortcuts.

settings.question: Is coconut water powder just marketing?
settings.answer:
Not the way we use it.

It adds natural minerals and a clean flavor backbone without turning this into a sugar drink. Coconut water alone is not enough for serious training, which is why it supports the formula instead of pretending to be the star.

settings.question: Can I drink this every day?
settings.answer:
Yes if your lifestyle earns it.

Hydration should scale with effort. Train harder, sweat more, replace more. This is not magic. It is math.

settings.heading: Why We Made It This Way
settings.subheading: Real answers about what's in the stick and why.
